This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operators
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module (SP-BEAM) Operator is a whitelisted entity that can directly alter the current parameters of vault types or savings rates that have been added to the SP-BEAM. Changes to rates are limited by the `max`, `min`, `step`, and `tau` parameters. Operators can be added or removed by an Executive Vote.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Operator Multisig is the Operator of the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module and is controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The address of the Operator Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xe1c6f81D0c3CD570A77813b81AA064c5fff80309`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Required Number Of Signers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Operator Multisig currently has a 2/3 signing requirement.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Signers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The signers of the Operator Multisig are three (3) addresses controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Usage Standards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The signers of the Operator Multisig must use the multisig to operate the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module in accordance with the instructions specified in [A.3.7.1.2.6 - Update Process](823aa477-5400-40e5-881f-acb9cf724c21).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis can change the signers of the Operator Multisig at any time, so long as there are at least three (3) signers and at least a majority of signers are required to execute transactions.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Update Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module Operators can be modified by the Stability Facilitators, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The rates set by the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module (SP-BEAM) are managed by the SP-BEAM Operators. The SP-BEAM Operators' use of this authority must wholly adhere to instructions provided by the Core Council Risk Advisor.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Request By Core Council Risk Advisor
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Any requests to change rates set by the SP-BEAM must be posted to the Sky Forum by the Core Council Risk Advisor that is recommending the change.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Execution
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once a rate change request has been posted to the Sky Forum by the Core Council Risk Advisor, the SP-BEAM Operators must prepare and execute the proposed changes in a timely manner. Preparation can include the creation of transaction simulations to verify inputs.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Public Communication
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once a change in rates set by the SP-BEAM has been executed, the execution must be publicly communicated to the Sky Ecosystem. This communication may take the form of either (1) a Forum post or (2) inclusion of the execution in an informational dashboard that allows community members to see each rate change executed by the SP-BEAM.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Keepers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Keeper networks provide critical services for the back-end systems of Sky, enhancing protocol security and efficiency. There are multiple providers of keeper networks in the Sky Ecosystem, decentralizing the keepers and ensuring redundancy. The subdocuments herein specify ongoing payment streams to compensate the different providers of keeper networks.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Gelato Network
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Gelato Network

Budget: 1,500 USDS per day
Stream Duration: 3 years (start date 29 May 2023).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Keep3r Network
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Keep3r Network

Budget: 1,500 USDS per day
Stream Duration: 3 years (start date 29 May 2023).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Chainlink Automation
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Chainlink Automation

Budget: 1,500 USDS per day
Stream Duration: 3 years (start date 29 May 2023).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Offboarding Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The subdocuments herein define the process by which Sky vault users should be notified about collateral offboarding.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Legacy Context
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Periodically, collateral and vault types (ilks) are re-evaluated, which sometimes results in an offboarding of that collateral or vault type. 

There have been several instances where users entered official Sky forums/chats to understand why they had been suddenly liquidated, despite historically comfortable collateralization ratios ([https://forum.sky.money/t/an-assessment-first-hand-experience-and-recommendations-from-the-aave-offboarding/11836](https://forum.sky.money/t/an-assessment-first-hand-experience-and-recommendations-from-the-aave-offboarding/11836)). This is, understandably, a poor user experience. 

Even with liquidation penalties set to 0%, the forced unwinding of levered positions conceivably can result in user losses, as well as potentially taxable events. 

This Section defines procedures concerning the dissemination of information about upcoming collateral offboarding on a best-effort basis. The objective is to minimize the number of users who are unaware of the offboarding prior to getting their positions liquidated.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Legacy Context Specific Goals
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Specific Goals:

1. Codify a series of good-faith communication efforts.

2. Provide an easy-to-follow process for Sky contributors to follow without significant burden.

3. Minimize the number of users unaware of a collateral offboarding.

4. Provide a method to bypass these requirements in the event a collateral is being offboarded due to serious security or solvency concerns.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Technical Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The subdocuments herein specify actions to be taken by specific actors within Sky when offboarding a vault type.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Immediate Actions
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Upon approval by Sky Governance to offboard a permissionless collateral type or vault type, the following actions are taken as soon as is practicable:

• Set debt ceiling to zero (0) Dai or USDS.

• Make an initial public announcement on all communication channels listed in [A.3.7.1.4.3.0.6.1 - Communication Channels And Media Assets Listing](9ac8a70b-8b6a-4825-a5e0-5e9019e50bc4).

• Make a second public announcement on all communication channels in the above cited document no later than 14 calendar days after the initial public announcement.

• Once the second public announcements have been made, set the liquidation penalty to 0%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Following Actions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Parameter changes designed to offboard users, such as changes to the liquidation ratio or stability fee, can only be implemented once 14 calendar days have passed following a second public announcement.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Communication Channels And Media Assets
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The process for adding, removing, and modifying communication channels and media assets, and the order of announcement publication, are defined as Active Data in [A.3.7.1.4.3.0.6.1 - Communication Channels And Media Assets Listing](9ac8a70b-8b6a-4825-a5e0-5e9019e50bc4).

The Active Data is updated as follows:

• The Responsible Party is Core GovOps.

• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

active_data.name: Communication Channels And Media Assets Listing
active_data.content:
The following channels should be used for initial and second public announcements of collateral offboarding. The order of announcement publication is as follows:

1. The author of the collateral offboarding notice shall post to the Sky Forum detailing the recommended offboarding. This forum thread is used to inform downstream announcements on other channels.

2. Core GovOps publishes the associated notices to:

• All public Sky Calendars

• The Sky Official Discord’s Announcement channel.

• The official Sky subreddit.

3. Partner Relationship Leads from relevant Ecosystem Actors reach out to:

• Affected collateral partners (e.g., Aave)

• Affected frontend service providers (e.g., Oasis, DeFi Saver)

Additionally, outreach may be done to cover more channels not listed here. These may include other Ecosystem Actor or Facilitator owned Twitter accounts, communication channels, and platforms.

Unless otherwise noted in [A.3.7.1.4 - Offboarding Process](3da8a0fd-952c-4f80-b674-f60c9a293cb9) and its subdocuments, Core GovOps will take the lead in coordinating channels announcements.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Expedited Offboarding
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The subdocuments herein define an expedited offboarding process in the event a collateral type needs to be removed quickly due to security or solvency concerns.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Requirements
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
When a collateral type threatens the security or solvency of the Sky Protocol, an expedited offboarding is in order. Expedited offboardings override the timeline specified in [A.3.7.1.4.2 - Technical Process](305e2bd6-a594-4aec-8713-adbe7bc87120) and allow the immediate modification of all relevant parameters.

Expedited offboardings must proceed pursuant to the following requirements:

• The Offboarding Proposal must state that the offboarding should be expedited due to an emergency or urgent concern, as defined in [A.1.8.1.1 - Definition Of Emergency Situations](5eafb29e-84a0-4a53-a798-3f958c880225)**.**

• The Offboarding Proposal must explicitly reference this Atlas document.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Announcements
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The public announcements defined in [A.3.7.1.4.2 - Technical Process](305e2bd6-a594-4aec-8713-adbe7bc87120) must still be carried out. However, the timing of these announcements no longer constrains the modification of parameters. These announcements must also state that the offboarding is being expedited and explain the reasons for this accelerated process.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Genesis Capital Backstop
sections_and_primary_docs.content: If a “SKY Backstop Event” is in progress and the SKY price equals zero, the Sky Protocol must apply a pro‑rata haircut across each Genesis Agent’s Genesis Capital, implemented as a transfer of eligible assets from each Agent to the Sky Surplus Buffer. The haircut must be sized exactly to restore the Surplus Buffer to zero.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Implementation
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
In the near term, transfers are made through Executive Votes and may be made through Emergency Spells (see [A.1.9.5 - Emergency Spells](b8266c11-3a84-4bbe-abe2-de9474f74ffd)) as part of the Emergency Response System (see [A.1.8.1 - Emergency Response](20dcf582-8862-48b3-9ca9-c3703871bd14)).

A solution must be developed to allow these transfers to be accomplished on an automated basis without waiting for the GSM Pause Delay (see [A.1.9.3 - Governance Security Delay Requirements](c5f0e955-0441-42e0-a6fc-eab875bba568)).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Post Backstop Settlement
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
If the haircut restores the Surplus Buffer to zero (i.e., the deficit is fully cleared), Sky will distribute 24 billion SKY to Genesis Agents, in proportion to the amount each Agent lost in the haircut.

If, after the haircut, the Surplus Buffer remains below zero (i.e., the deficit could not be fully cleared), Sky will adjust the USDS target price below $1 to the extent necessary to settle the remaining deficit; Sky will then distribute 24 billion SKY to USDS holders via an airdrop.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Genesis Agent Capital Shortfalls
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Transfers of capital from Genesis Agents may cause capital shortfalls for Genesis Agents under the Risk Framework (see [A.3.2 - Risk Capital](55999acf-75fe-4adf-8584-9746ef50d3e4)). Sky will work in good faith with such Agents to waive penalties for a reasonable period of time to allow the Agents to rebuild their capital.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Relation To SKY Backstop
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Genesis Capital Backstop may not be invoked unless a “SKY Backstop Event” is in progress and the price of SKY equals zero.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Genesis Agents
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Genesis Agents are Agents that receive capital contributions from Sky, including, without limitation:
    
◦ Spark    
◦ Grove    
◦ Keel    
◦ Launch Agent 3

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Genesis Capital
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Genesis Capital of an Agent is the lesser of (1) the amount of capital contributed by Sky to the Agent and (2) the total capital of the Agent.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Amount Of Capital Contributed By Sky To Agents
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The amount of capital contributed by Sky to Agents is:

◦ Spark - 25,000,000 USDS

name: The Protocol Scope
content: The Protocol Scope regulates the maintenance and development of the core Sky Protocol and its critical, non-collateral components. The Protocol Scope defines all rules for protocol engineering.

articles.name: Core Tokens
articles.content: The two core tokens, USDS and SKY, play the central role in the usability and tokenomics of the Sky Ecosystem.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: USDS
sections_and_primary_docs.content: USDS is the Stablecoin product of the Sky Protocol. It is designed to remain stable against USD, and its supply is regulated through the Peg Stability Module and the Allocation System, as governed by the Stability Scope.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: USDS Launch
sections_and_primary_docs.content: In the Endgame Token Launch Phase, USDS is launched as an upgrade to Dai, offering new features, including Token Rewards. Dai can be exchanged to and from USDS at a rate of 1:1.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY
sections_and_primary_docs.content: SKY is the governance token of the Sky Protocol. It grants voting rights in the Sky Governance system. Its liquidity is boosted by the Smart Burn Engine, as governed by the Stability Scope.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Historically, MKR was the governance token of the Sky Protocol. As part of the transition to the Endgame, the technical infrastructure of the Sky Protocol must be upgraded to use SKY as its governance token rather than MKR. The upgrade will take place over three phases, as defined in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Approval
sections_and_primary_docs.content: **MKR holders have voted to approve the deprecation of MKR as the governance token of the Sky Protocol with full knowledge of the changes being proposed. See **[**https://vote.makerdao.com/polling/QmcZNZg3**](https://vote.makerdao.com/polling/QmcZNZg3)**. This deprecation includes all actions defined in **[**A.4.1.2.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade**](6bb8b5b2-a2a8-4728-bddb-28bf054de9b6)**, including, but not limited to, the removal of voting rights from MKR and the Delayed Upgrade Penalty.**

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase One
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define Phase One of the upgrade from MKR to SKY as the governance token of the Protocol.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase One Timing
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Phase One is expected to occur in the May 15, 2025 Executive Vote.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase One Actions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The actions specified in the documents herein will be taken as part of Phase One. These actions are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Disable Conversion From SKY To MKR In Existing Conversion Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The functionality to convert SKY to MKR in the existing converter contract located at `0xBDcFCA946b6CDd965f99a839e4435Bcdc1bc470B` on the Ethereum Mainnet must be disabled.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Launch New MKR To SKY Conversion Contract
Disable MKR Flop Auctions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: MKR flop auctions must be disabled.

annotations.name: MKR Flop Auctions - Element Annotation
annotations.content: The element “MKR flop auctions” refers to auctions in which the Sky Protocol issues and auctions off MKR in exchange for Dai. These auctions allow the Sky Protocol to recapitalize itself to offset net debt.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Disable Emergency Shutdown Module
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Emergency Shutdown Module must be disabled by setting the amount of MKR required to trigger it to infinity.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Launch New Chief Contract

annotations.name: Chief Contract - Element Annotation
annotations.content: The element “Chief Contract” refers to the contract that implements the Sky Protocol’s on-chain voting mechanism. Under this mechanism, the hat, or current executive authority, is elected via token-weighted votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Move Authority Of Spark Freezer MOM Contract To New Chief Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: A Spark Spell must be triggered to move the authority of the Spark Freezer MOM contract to the new Chief contract. See [A.1.9.3.2.7 - SparkLend Freezer Mom Exception](645443f1-9004-48d3-868c-139b608bb1f9).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Launch New SKY Oracle
sections_and_primary_docs.content: A new Oracle Security Module based on the Scribe SKY oracle must be deployed.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Add New SKY Oracle to Oracle Security Module MOM Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The new SKY oracle must be added to the OSM_MOM contract. See [A.1.9.3.2.2 - Oracle Freeze Exception](e604c477-e8e4-483f-a41e-c93dcff3acfd).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Smart Burn Engine Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The parameters of the Smart Burn Engine must be updated as follows:
    
◦ The oracle must be changed to the new SKY oracle; and    
◦ The `rewardsDuration` for the `LSEV2-SKY-A` USDS rewards contract must be set such that it is equal to the `splitter.hop` parameter.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Launch SKY Staking
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
SKY Staking must be launched with the following parameters for borrowing against staked SKY:
    
◦ `Ilk`: LSEV2-SKY-A    
◦ `Farms`: 
    ◦ LSSKY → USDS (`0x38E4254bD82ED5Ee97CD1C4278FAae748d998865`)
    ◦ LSSKY → SPK (`0x99cBC0e4E6427F6939536eD24d1275B95ff77404`)   
◦ `Fee`: 0    
◦ DC-IAM `line`: Set at discretion of Stability Facilitators in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor
◦ DC-IAM `gap`: Set at discretion of Stability Facilitators in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor
◦ DC-IAM `ttl`: Set at discretion of Stability Facilitators in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor
◦ Only existing LSE-MKR-A users with drawn debt are allowed to migrate debt to LSEV2-SKY-A initially    
◦ `Dust`: 30,000    
◦ `Duty`: 20%    
◦ `Mat`: 145%    
◦ `Calc`: StairstepExponentialDecrease    
◦ `Buf`: 120%    
◦ `Tail`: 6,000 seconds    
◦ `Cusp`: 40%    
◦ `Chip`: 0.1%    
◦ `Tip`: 300 USDS    
◦ `Stopped`: 3    
◦ `Chop`: 13%    
◦ `Hole`: 250,000    
◦ `Tau`: 0 days    
◦ `Cut`: 0.99    
◦ `Step`: 60 seconds    
◦ `LineMom`: Yes    
◦ `Tolerance`: 0.5    
◦ `Name`: LockstakeSky    
◦ `Symbol`: lsSKY    
◦ Use new SKY Chronicle Scribe oracle instance OSM    
◦ Deny the CLIPPER_MOM from the new lockstake clipper

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Deploy New Vote Delegate Factory Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: A new Vote Delegate factory contract must be deployed. The Vote Delegate factory contract must support deploying Delegate Contracts to which SKY holders and SKY Staking users can delegate their voting power.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Two
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define Phase Two of the upgrade from MKR to SKY as the governance token of the Protocol.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Two Timing
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Phase Two is expected to occur in the May 29, 2025 Executive Vote.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Two Actions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The actions specified in the documents herein will be taken as part of Phase Two. These actions are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Parameters For Staked Sky
Adjust GSM Pause Delay
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The GSM Pause Delay must be set to 24 hours. See [A.1.9.3.1.2 - Pause Delay Current Value](db442d8a-8d98-47a2-b162-01c2adc22b67).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Revoke Migrator Authority Over Vat Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Migrator contract authority over the Vat contract must be revoked. This authority was necessary for existing users of `LSE-MKR-A` to migrate to `LSEV2-SKY-A` but is no longer required.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Three
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define Phase Three of the upgrade from MKR to SKY as the governance token of the Protocol.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Three Timing
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Phase Three will occur in Executive Votes subsequent to the May 29, 2025 Executive Vote. Unlike Phases One and Two, Phase Three will occur over multiple future Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Phase Three Actions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The actions specified in the documents herein will be taken as part of Phase Three. These actions are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Develop SKY Flop Auctions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Functionality for SKY flop auctions must be developed.

annotations.name: SKY Flop Auctions - Element Annotation
annotations.content: The element “SKY flop auctions” refers to auctions in which the Sky Protocol issues and auctions off SKY in exchange for USDS. These auctions allow the Sky Protocol to recapitalize itself to offset net debt.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Offboard Borrowing Against Staked MKR
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Seal Engine (LSE-MKR-A) must be offboarded as a collateral type. This will require retiring the existing SKY wrapped oracle and removing it from the MegaPoker keeper as well as other actions that will be identified at a future date.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Upgrade MKR In Pause Proxy To SKY
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The MKR in the Pause Proxy contract must be upgraded to SKY.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Liquidation Parameters For Borrowing Against Staked SKY
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once sufficient liquidity has been attained, liquidations must be enabled and parameters for LSEV2-SKY-A must be updated to normal levels.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Set Conversion Fee In MKR To SKY Conversion Contract To 1%
sections_and_primary_docs.content: In the September 18, 2025 Executive Vote, the Delayed Upgrade Penalty for the new MKR to SKY conversion contract (see [A.4.1.2.1.2.2.2 - Launch New MKR To SKY Conversion Contract](17677358-fe08-491c-a0c3-25ab63ed0eaa)) must be set to 1%. Thereafter, the Delayed Upgrade Penalty must be increased gradually at the rate of 1 percentage point per three months.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Upgrade Modification Of Phases
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The timing and contents of Phase One, Phase Two, and Phase Three may be adjusted by the Protocol Facilitators in consultation with relevant Ecosystem Actors.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Deflationary Tokenomics
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein set forth the core deflationary tokenomics of the SKY token.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Initial Token Supply
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The initial token supply of SKY tokens is derived from the total number of legacy MKR tokens as of the launch of Sky, multiplied by the conversion ratio of 24,000. SKY is the exclusive governance token of the Sky Protocol and takes over all governance and economic rights previously held by MKR. See [A.4.1.2.1 - MKR To SKY Upgrade](6bb8b5b2-a2a8-4728-bddb-28bf054de9b6).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: No New Token Emissions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: No new SKY tokens may be emitted except for (1) emissions required to recapitalize the protocol if it is at risk of insolvency (see [A.3.6 - SKY Backstop](4d8b0d82-97da-4041-b185-4b98c2779cbe)) and (2) temporary emissions that are being deprecated and fully offset by corresponding burns, resulting in no net increase to the intended long-term token supply (see [A.4.1.2.2.4 - Deprecated Emissions Mechanisms](2f3962e4-c79e-4583-82df-31239dfb84a4)).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Burning Of Existing Tokens
sections_and_primary_docs.content: At the same time that no new SKY tokens are issued, SKY tokens are bought back and burned on a regular basis, using a portion of the Net Revenue of the Protocol as dictated by the Treasury Management Function. See [A.2.4.1.2.5.1 - Smart Burn Buffer](c2a0ca01-ffea-46ee-8077-e46e0510d097). As a result, the total supply of SKY tokens will continue to decrease over time.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Deprecated Emissions Mechanisms
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The subdocuments herein define legacy mechanisms involving the issuance of new SKY tokens. Each of these mechanisms is being deprecated. While these mechanisms do not increase the net supply of SKY tokens, they must be replaced with solutions that do not involve any emissions of SKY tokens, as defined in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Token Rewards Emissions
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
USDS users are able to earn SKY Rewards. See [A.4.3.2 - Token Reward Mechanism](3ff5a7b2-db91-41ab-be19-ddb068b36cc7). These rewards are currently funded by emissions of new SKY tokens.

These ongoing emissions will be eliminated and replaced with a solution that funds these rewards using SKY held by the Sky Protocol.

At the same time that this new solution is implemented, SKY tokens equal to the total SKY emissions previously used to fund SKY Rewards, including the Early Bird Reward (see [A.5.3.1.1.1 - Token Distribution](eaf8cf29-90fd-4b9b-b0a8-02ce8386908c)), will be burned from SKY held by the Sky Protocol.

The combination of these actions will achieve a state where the number of total SKY tokens is exactly the same as if SKY Rewards had never been funded with protocol emissions.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: MKR To SKY Conversion Emissions
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The legacy MKR to SKY conversion contract (Legacy Conversion Contract) burns MKR and mints new SKY. The new MKR to SKY conversion contract (New Conversion Contract) instead burns MKR and issues SKY from the existing supply of preminted SKY.

The Legacy Conversion Contract will be disabled as specified in [A.4.1.2.2.4.2.1 - Disabling Legacy Conversion Contract](1b8248bf-5d88-4d67-8c4a-21981a0aa937). To offset SKY minted by the Legacy Conversion Contract after the deployment of the New Conversion Contract, an equivalent amount of preminted SKY will be burned. This action will ensure the total SKY token supply remains as if these specific emissions from the Legacy Conversion Contract had not occurred.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Disabling Legacy Conversion Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The disabling of the Legacy Conversion Contract will be executed in the June 26, 2025 Executive Vote. This action is authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

articles.name: SkyLink
articles.content: SkyLink is a multichain system that enables native crosschain transfer of Sky Ecosystem-related tokens to other blockchains, including Ethereum L2s and major L1s.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Multichain Support Native Mechanisms
sections_and_primary_docs.content: SkyLink deployments support features including the Savings Rate Mechanism (including sUSDS), the Token Rewards Mechanism and the SKY Staking Mechanism.

articles.name: Savings Rate And Token Reward Mechanism
articles.content: This Article regulates the rewards benefitting Dai users and USDS users for holding each Stablecoin. DAI users can access the legacy DAI Savings Rate Mechanism. USDS users can access a built-in Savings Rate, as well as additional Token Reward Mechanisms including SKY and Agent tokens.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Savings Rates
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Savings Rate Mechanism includes both the legacy Dai Savings Rate Mechanism and the Sky Savings Rate. The Sky Savings Rate includes a built-in sUSDS mechanism. The Savings Rate is governed by the Stability Scope.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Token Reward Mechanism
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Token Rewards Mechanism provides SKY rewards to USDS users. Agent tokens are also distributed through a Token Rewards Mechanism.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Token Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
With each Executive Vote, the distribution of SKY token rewards to USDS holders is normalized to ensure the effective yield provided by these rewards equals the Sky Savings Rate (SSR) for the upcoming period. The effective yield is defined as: (Total Market Value of Distributed SKY Tokens / Total Eligible USDS Balance).

To achieve this normalization, the quantity of SKY token rewards to be distributed is determined through the following calculation, performed for each normalization period:

1. Determine Target Reward Value: Calculate the total US dollar value required to meet the SSR for the period: (Total Eligible USDS Balance * SSR).
    
2. Determine SKY Token Quantity: Divide the Target Reward Value (from step 1) by the Current Market Price of SKY (determined at the time of the Executive Vote).

The Core Facilitator, on the recommendation of the Core Council Risk Advisor, may maintain the quantity of SKY token rewards to be distributed for a normalization period at its current level if they conclude that:
    
◦ the difference between the effective yield provided by SKY Tokens Rewards and the SSR is not material;
    
◦ the difference between the effective yield provided by SKY Token Rewards and the SSR is unlikely to persist due to other factors; or
    
◦ adjusting the quantity of SKY Token Rewards is otherwise inadvisable.

The recommendation of the Core Council Risk Advisor must be posted to the Sky Forum.

articles.name: SKY Staking Mechanism
articles.content: This Article governs the SKY Staking Mechanism that grants voting rewards on staked SKY tokens and its associated SKY-backed borrowing mechanism funded via the stUSDS token.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Staking
sections_and_primary_docs.content: SKY holders can stake their tokens via the SKY Staking Mechanism available on Ethereum Mainnet and SkyLink Deployments. SKY stakers earn voting rewards sourced from the Sky Treasury Management Function. In lieu of USDS rewards and SKY rewards, SKY stakers can choose to earn Agent Token Rewards, including SPK and the tokens of other Prime Agents incubated by Sky. SKY stakers can also borrow USDS against their staked collateral using the SKY-backed borrowing mechanism defined herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Staking Voting Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define the SKY Staking Voting Rewards. SKY stakers may choose between receiving USDS or Agent Token rewards.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Sources of Voting Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: SKY stakers are eligible to receive voting rewards sourced from the Sky Treasury Management Function (TMF) or the Agent Token Distribution mechanism defined in [A.4.5 - Distribution Of Agent Tokens](e2f1f01f-3303-41c3-b337-f09eb41ba6be).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Treasury Management Function-Derived Voting Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: USDS rewards are distributed via two distinct mechanisms based on the staker’s activity level: the High Activity Staking Reward (HASR) and the Standard Activity Staking Reward (SASR). SASR is the base reward when funding from the Treasury Management Function is available; HASR is an additional boost reserved for High-Activity wallets. See [A.2.4.1.2.3.3 - Payout From High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer](6e857f3d-1e5c-4a47-943d-439b1d0d87ea).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Activity Levels
sections_and_primary_docs.content: At the Monthly Settlement Cycle, all SKY-staking wallets are tagged as High Activity or Standard Activity, pursuant to the criteria defined in [A.2.4.1.2.3.4 - High Activity Staker Qualification](8e83f80f-5606-41a7-bc90-d79df38b55fe) and [A.2.4.1.2.5.2.3 - Standard Activity Staker Qualification](a22d900a-d15c-41f0-af90-eec23112dcb6). Only High Activity wallets share HASR; both High and Standard Activity wallets share SASR.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: High Activity Staking Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The High Activity Staking Rewards (HASR) is a boosted reward provided to High Activity wallets. The HASR amount is distributed each monthly cycle pro-rata to the SKY stake of all High Activity wallets. High Activity wallets receive SASR (when funding is available) plus their separate HASR boost.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Standard Activity Staking Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Standard Activity Staking Rewards (SASR) is the base reward shared by all wallets with Standard Activity status or higher. When funding is available, the SASR amount is distributed each monthly cycle pro‑rata to the SKY stake of every Standard or High Activity wallet.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Agent Token Distribution Voting Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: SKY stakers are eligible to receive Agent Token rewards as specified in [A.4.5 - Distribution Of Agent Tokens](e2f1f01f-3303-41c3-b337-f09eb41ba6be). At the Monthly Settlement Cycle, Agent Tokens are distributed pro‑rata, by staked SKY, among wallets that have opted to receive a specific Agent Token for the cycle. To qualify for Agent Token payout a wallet must satisfy the Standard Activity criteria specified in [A.2.4.1.2.5.2.2 - Payout From Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer](e288b944-759e-4d30-8db7-b59de7b5f912).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY-Backed Borrowing
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
SKY stakers can borrow USDS against their staked SKY collateral. This borrowing will be facilitated exclusively by the stUSDS system which will provide protocol-independent, or segregated, risk capital. The stUSDS system is currently in development; its elements are outlined in the subdocuments below.

During the transition period to the stUSDS system, the protocol-dependent SKY-backed borrowing system remains operational and is governed by [A.4.4.1.3 - Short Term Transitionary Measures](22b8f8bf-b477-4439-86f7-ec605d3c657a).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Function
sections_and_primary_docs.content: stUSDS is a yield-bearing token representing USDS deposited into the stUSDS contract. Its primary function is to provide segregated risk capital for the SKY-backed borrowing mechanism. Users convert USDS to stUSDS to provide this capital and earn the stUSDS Rate. See [A.4.4.1.2.2 - stUSDS Rate](7e51d5a7-0707-4fba-999b-a1becd5f0192). stUSDS can be converted back to USDS subject to available unutilized liquidity in the stUSDS converter contract.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Rate
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The variable yield earned by stUSDS holders is calculated using the formula:

`stUSDS Rate = Sky Savings Rate + (SKY Borrow Rate - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate) * Utilization - Rfactor * f(Utilization)`

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Parameters Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the stUSDS Rate formula are further defined in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Sky Savings Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Sky Savings Rate` is the Sky Savings Rate defined in [A.3.1.2.2 - Sky Savings Rate](2674cccb-d779-4868-b83f-8cb86648c88a).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Rate` is the SKY Borrow Rate defined in [A.4.4.1.2.5 - SKY Borrow Rate](5e546766-a0c0-4744-9ca9-5509db14bc30).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Minimum Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate defined in [A.4.4.1.2.5.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](6e329dd6-eda5-43ce-9899-b3a03ede8d0b).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Utilization Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Utilization` is the percent of funds in the stUSDS contract that are used to fund borrowing against staked SKY.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Utilization Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `Utilization` is calculated as a time-weighted utilization over a one (1) day interval.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Time-Weighted Utilization
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define the process to calculate time-weighted utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Determine Start And End Times
sections_and_primary_docs.content: First, the start time $T_{0}$ and end time $T_{n}$ of the interval, over which time-weighted utilization are to be calculated, are selected.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Collect Borrow And Supply Events
Insert Synthetic Events
sections_and_primary_docs.content: If $t_{0}$ is greater than $T_{0}$ then a synthetic event is inserted at $T_{0}$ with values $B_{0}$ and $S_{0}$. Similarly, if $t_{n}$ is less than $T_{n}$ then a synthetic event is inserted at $T_{n}$ with values $B_{n}$ and $S_{n}$.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Calculate Time-Weighted Utilization
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Finally, the time-weighted utilization is calculated using the following formula:

$$\text{Util}_{\mathrm{avg}} = \frac{\sum_{i=0}^{n-1} U_i \cdot (t_{i+1} - t_i)}{T_n - T_0}$$

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Reference Implementation
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
A reference implementation of the time-weighted utilization formula is included herein. The reference implementation uses sample data and a 30 day interval for illustrative purposes.

`# Your list of real events: (timestamp, total_borrowed, total_supply)
events = [
(datetime(2025, 7, 25, 10), 100_000, 200_000),
(datetime(2025, 7, 25, 16), 120_000, 210_000),
(datetime(2025, 7, 26, 8), 130_000, 220_000),
(datetime(2025, 7, 27, 12), 110_000, 215_000),
(datetime(2025, 7, 28, 9), 140_000, 225_000),
]

# Ensure events are sorted
events.sort()

# Get time window
now = datetime.utcnow()
start_time = now - timedelta(days=30)

# Add synthetic first event (30d ago) using earliest known borrow/supply
first_real_ts, first_borrow, first_supply = events[0]
if first_real_ts > start_time:
events.insert(0, (start_time, first_borrow, first_supply))

# Add synthetic final event (now) using most recent known borrow/supply
last_real_ts, last_borrow, last_supply = events[-1]
if last_real_ts < now:
events.append((now, last_borrow, last_supply))

# Step 1: Compute utilization per event
utilizations = []
for ts, borrow, supply in events:
utilization = borrow / supply if supply != 0 else 0
utilizations.append((ts, utilization))

# Step 2: Compute time-weighted utilization average
weighted_sum = 0
total_time = 0

for i in range(len(utilizations) - 1):
ts1, util1 = utilizations[i]
ts2, _ = utilizations[i + 1]

time_diff = (ts2 - ts1).total_seconds()
weighted_sum += util1 * time_diff
total_time += time_diff

avg_utilization = weighted_sum / total_time if total_time > 0 else 0

print(f"30-day time-weighted average utilization: {avg_utilization:.2%}")`

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rfactor Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
`Rfactor` is calculated using the formula:

$$
Rfactor = \frac{2u_m - 1}{2u_m \left( u_{opt}(\alpha + 1) - 1 + \frac{\beta u_{opt}}{slope1} \right)}
$$

The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Maximum Profit Utilization Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: $u_{m}$ is the maximum profit utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Maximum Profit Utilization Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The current value of $u_{m}$ is 70%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Target Utilization Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: $u_{opt}$ is the target utilization specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.1 - Rate Setting Mechanism](5ad3e32c-9b5c-431a-bc20-e236194b65e8).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Alpha Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
$\alpha$ is calculated using the formula:

`alpha = ((SKY Borrow Maximum Rate - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate) / Slope 1) - 1`

The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Maximum Rate` is the maximum value of the SKY Borrow Rate at 100% Utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The current value of the `SKY Borrow Maximum Rate` is 30%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Minimum Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](6e329dd6-eda5-43ce-9899-b3a03ede8d0b).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 1 Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Slope 1` is the Slope 1 parameter specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.4 - Slope 1](f22da959-a76e-477a-a87b-a32c429d2ec0).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Beta Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: $\beta$ is a tuning parameter that determines how much profit is made at maximum utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Beta Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The current value of $\beta$ is 100%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 1 Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: $slope1$ is the Slope 1 parameter specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.4 - Slope 1](f22da959-a76e-477a-a87b-a32c429d2ec0).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Utilization Function Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
`f(Utilization)` is calculated using the formula:

`f(Utilization) = Utilization * ((SKY Borrow Maximum Rate - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate + Beta) * Utilization + SKY Borrow Minimum Rate - SKY Borrow Rate)`

The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Utilization Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Utilization` is the utilization as specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4 - Utilization Definition](337c4f67-685f-42bd-8237-553ed913b89f).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Maximum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Maximum Rate specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3.1 - SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Definition](eb77a744-db25-40b0-b51c-dd3187941cc5).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Minimum Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](6e329dd6-eda5-43ce-9899-b3a03ede8d0b).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Beta Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Beta` is the beta specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.4 - Beta Definition](182418ba-47ad-416b-a5fe-440ac92511ec).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Rate` is the SKY Borrow Rate specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5 - SKY Borrow Rate](5e546766-a0c0-4744-9ca9-5509db14bc30).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Parameters Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2.1 - Parameters Definition](b9a9d09d-57c5-42cd-994f-f5689996f635) that are set by governance may be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Holders’ Risk Bearing
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Holders of stUSDS accept the risk associated with providing capital for SKY-backed borrowing. In the event that the liquidation of a borrower’s staked SKY collateral does not cover the outstanding debt, stUSDS balances will be subject to a haircut proportional to the shortfall. This mechanism ensures that the Sky Protocol is isolated from losses originating from the SKY-backed borrowing facility.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Debt Ceiling
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The maximum amount of USDS that can be borrowed against staked SKY is dynamically determined by, and equal to, the total amount of USDS currently held within the stUSDS contract. This dynamic ceiling will replace the static `DC-IAM` module and parameters associated with protocol-dependent, SKY-backed borrowing.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Rate
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The interest rate charged to borrowers (SKY Borrow Rate) is dynamic and market-driven, based on the utilization of funds within the stUSDS contract. This dynamic rate will replace the static `Stability Fee` parameter associated with protocol-dependent, SKY-backed borrowing.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rate Setting Mechanism
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The SKY Borrow Rate adjusts to target a 90% utilization rate of the USDS in the stUSDS contract. When stUSDS utilization is below 90%, the rate gradually decreases; when above 90%, it gradually increases. The SKY Borrow Rate cannot fall below the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate. See [A.4.4.1.2.5.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](6e329dd6-eda5-43ce-9899-b3a03ede8d0b).

The specific parameters and formula governing the rate of adjustment are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rate Setting Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the rate setting mechanism are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Utilization
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Utilization` is the percentage of funds in the stUSDS contract that are used to fund borrowing against staked SKY specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4 - Utilization Definition](337c4f67-685f-42bd-8237-553ed913b89f).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Target Utilization
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Target Utilization` is the target utilization rate of USDS in the stUSDS contract specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.1 - Rate Setting Mechanism](5ad3e32c-9b5c-431a-bc20-e236194b65e8).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Minimum Rate
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the rate that the SKY Borrow Rate cannot fall below specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](6e329dd6-eda5-43ce-9899-b3a03ede8d0b).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 1
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Slope 1` represents the spread of the SKY Borrow Rate over the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate when Utilization is at Target Utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 1 Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The current value of the `Slope 1` parameter is 12.575%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 1 Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `Slope 1` parameter may be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 2
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Slope 2` represents the spread of the SKY Borrow Rate at 100% Utilization over the SKY Borrow Rate at Target Utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 2 Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The current value of the `Slope 2` parameter is 12.575%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 2 Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `Slope 2` parameter may be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 2 Methodology
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Under normal circumstances governance should set the `Slope 2` parameter based on the following formula:

`Slope 2 = Slope 1 * alpha`

The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Slope 1 Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `Slope 1` parameter is specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.4 - Slope 1](f22da959-a76e-477a-a87b-a32c429d2ec0).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Alpha Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `alpha` parameter is specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3 - Alpha Definition](e9cfb75d-2d5b-448e-a3e1-784326a94ac4).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rate Setting Formula
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The Sky Borrow Rate is calculated according to the following formula when Utilization is less than or equal to Target Utilization:

`SKY Borrow Rate = SKY Borrow Minimum Rate + Utilization / Target Utilization * Slope 1`

The SKY Borrow Rate is calculated according to the following formula when Utilization is greater than Target Utilization:

`SKY Borrow Rate = SKY Borrow Minimum Rate + Slope 1 + (Utilization - Target Utilization) / (1 - Target Utilization) * Slope 2`

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY Borrow Minimum Rate
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The SKY Borrow Minimum Rate is calculated according to the following formula:

`SKY Borrow Minimum Rate = Sky Savings Rate + stUSDS Accessibility Reward`

The formula ensures the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate covers the baseline cost of capital (Sky Savings Rate) plus the incentive cost (stUSDS Accessibility Reward), preventing value-draining arbitrage where users could borrow below the Sky Savings Rate.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Parameters Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate formula are further defined in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Sky Savings Rate Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `Sky Savings Rate` is defined in [A.3.1.2.2 - Sky Savings Rate](2674cccb-d779-4868-b83f-8cb86648c88a).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Accessibility Reward Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: `stUSDS Accessibility Reward` is defined in [A.4.4.1.2.7 - stUSDS Accessibility Reward](673676d8-62a4-4422-b870-fbcdb3c0aabd).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Risk Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The liquidation parameters for SKY-backed loans funded via stUSDS are:
 
◦ Liquidation Ratio: 125%    
◦ `Calc`: StairstepExponentialDecrease    
◦ `Tau`: 0 days
◦ `Tolerance`: 0.5
◦ `Cut`: 0.99
◦ `Step`: 60 seconds
◦ `Buf`: 120%    
◦ `Cusp`: 40%
◦ `Tail`: 6,000 seconds
◦ `Chip`: 0.1%
◦ `Stopped`: 3
◦ `Tip`: 300 USDS    
◦ `Chop`: 13%    
◦ `Hole`: 250,000    
◦ `Dust`: 30,000

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Modification Of stUSDS Risk Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Except as specified in [A.4.4.1.2.6.1.1 - Modification Of stUSDS Auction Parameters](0296f17f-b615-4f93-9505-94bd78c24324), the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor, have the ability to modify any of the parameters defined in [A.4.4.1.2.6 - stUSDS Risk Parameters](fac38a01-4c67-4810-af22-3e7b2d855567). The modification of said parameters is pursuant to the Operational Weekly Cycle and can be effected directly via an Executive Vote, without requiring a Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Modification Of stUSDS Auction Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: A clear justification and analysis must be provided to validate any proposed changes to the `Calc`, `Tau`, `Buf`, `Cusp`, `Tail`, `Chip`, `Tip`, `Chop`, or `Hole` parameters. Before these changes are added to an Executive Vote, the Core Executor Agents must obtain approval through a Governance Poll. However, in an emergency, the Core Executor Agents have the authority to bypass the Governance Poll and add the proposed parameters directly to an Executive Vote. These parameters must be regularly monitored and updated if needed.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Accessibility Reward
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The stUSDS Accessibility Reward is an incentive mechanism, similar to the USDS Accessibility Reward, designed to encourage Prime Agents and Integrators to promote stUSDS adoption. See [A.2.3.8.1 - Accessibility Reward Primitive](e632c38f-3e4e-4c7e-acfd-b6ec45a422e6). It is calculated as a percentage of the stUSDS balance associated with a Reward Code. The reward is initially set at 0.05% for the Integrator portion and 0.05% for the Prime Agent Management Fee, totaling 0.1%.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Bounded External Access Module
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The stUSDS Bounded External Access Module (stUSDS BEAM) enables designated, Governance-whitelisted operators to adjust the stUSDS Rate (`str`), the SKY Borrow Rate (`duty`), the maximum amount that users can deposit into the stUSDS contract (`cap`), and the maximum Debt Ceiling (`line`). Adjustments are governed by the stUSDS BEAM smart contract logic and specific parameters set by Sky Governance. stUSDS BEAM holds four parameters that can be set for each stUSDS parameter: (i) `min`, (ii) `max`, (iii) `step`, and (iv) `tau`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Definitions
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define the parameters of the stUSDS BEAM.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Min Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `min` parameter defines the minimum value for the `str` and `duty` parameters that can be set using the stUSDS BEAM. Each of the `str` and `duty` parameters has a specific `min`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Max Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `max` parameter defines the maximum value for the `str` and `duty` parameters that can be set using the stUSDS BEAM. Each of the `str` and `duty` parameters has a specific `max`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Step Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `step` parameter limits how much the `str` and `duty` parameters can be increased or decreased in a single transaction, bound by the `tau` parameter. Each of the `str` and `duty` parameters has a specific `step`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Max Cap Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `maxCap` parameter defines the maximum value for the `cap` parameter that can be set by the stUSDS BEAM.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Max Cap Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `maxCap` is currently set to 1,000,000,000 USDS.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Max Line Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `maxLine` parameter defines the maximum value for the `line` parameter that can be set by the stUSDS BEAM.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Max Line Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `maxLine` is currently set to 1,000,000,000 USDS.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Tau Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The `tau` parameter defines the minimum time interval, in seconds, that must elapse between consecutive uses or operations of the stUSDS BEAM.

A stUSDS BEAM operation may adjust one or more parameters. Once a stUSDS BEAM operation is executed, the `tau` duration must expire before any subsequent stUSDS BEAM operation can be performed.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Tau Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `tau` is currently set to 57,600 seconds (16 hours).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: stUSDS Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The stUSDS BEAM parameters for each stUSDS parameter set by the stUSDS BEAM are defined in the subdocuments herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Str Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The stUSDS BEAM parameters for the `str` stUSDS parameter are as follows:
    
◦ `max` - 5,000 basis points,    
◦ `min` - 200 basis points,    
◦ `step` - 4,000 basis points.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Duty Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The stUSDS BEAM parameters for the `duty` stUSDS parameter are as follows:
    
◦ `max` - 5,000 basis points,
◦ `min` - 210 basis points,    
◦ `step` - 4,000 basis points.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Parameter Adjustments
sections_and_primary_docs.content: All stUSDS BEAM parameters can be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operators
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The stUSDS BEAM Operators are whitelisted entities that can directly alter the stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM. Changes to stUSDS parameters are limited by the `max`, `min`, `step`, and `tau` parameters. Operators can be added or removed by an Executive Vote.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Operator Multisig is an Operator of the stUSDS BEAM and is controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The address of the Operator Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xBB865F94B8A92E57f79fCc89Dfd4dcf0D3fDEA16`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Required Number Of Signers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Operator Multisig currently has a 2/3 signing requirement.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Signers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The signers of the Operator Multisig are three (3) addresses controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Usage Standards
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The signers of the Operator Multisig must use the multisig to operate the stUSDS BEAM in accordance with the instructions specified in [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.2 - Manual Parameter Updates By Operator Multisig](944c2573-1184-4d6b-bbe9-0b84c11956cf).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Multisig Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis can change the signers of the Operator Multisig at any time, so long as there are at least three (3) signers and at least a majority of signers are required to execute transactions.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Hot Wallet
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Operator Hot Wallet is a hot wallet controlled by a bot that will update stUSDS parameters on an automated basis as specified in [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.3 - Automatic Updates By Operator Hot Wallet](a6e1735f-bd82-4ab6-982b-218013c3455f). The wallet is controlled by Ecosystem Actor TechOps Services under the supervision of Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. The addition of the Hot Wallet as an Operator of the stUSDS BEAM is authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Hot Wallet Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The address of the Operator Hot Wallet on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xd06C14820048de2Fb7c9de611EcFdaCE18eC8896`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Of stUSDS Parameters For Hot Wallet
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
When the Operator Hot Wallet is added as an Operator of the stUSDS Rate, the stUSDS BEAM parameters must be updated as follows:
    
◦ The `step` parameters for the `str` and `duty` parameters must be reduced to 400 basis points; and    
◦ The `tau` parameter must be reduced to 4 hours.

These changes are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Update Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: stUSDS BEAM Operators can be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM are managed by the stUSDS BEAM Operators as specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Initial Parameter Values Set In Executive Vote
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The initial parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM in the Executive Vote deploying stUSDS and the stUSDS BEAM are:
    
◦ `str` - 0 basis points    
◦ `duty` - 2,000 basis points    
◦ `cap` - 200,000,000 USDS    
◦ `line` - 200,000,000 USDS

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Manual Parameter Updates By Operator Multisig
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Initially, the stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM are managed by the Operator Multisig based on instructions provided by the Core Council Risk Advisor.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Request By Core Council Risk Advisor
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Any requests to change stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM must be posted to the Sky Forum by the Core Council Risk Advisor recommending the change. Once Utilization is below 100% the Core Council Risk Advisor should begin recommending changes based on the methodology specified in [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3 - Update Methodology](e37d1045-215d-4f85-bbc3-70aa2c1b818b) but may deviate from the values resulting from this methodology if they determine that other values are advisable to support the growth of stUSDS and the interests of the Sky Protocol.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Operator Execution
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once a request to change stUSDS parameters has been posted to the Sky Forum by the Core Council Risk Advisor, the stUSDS BEAM Operators must prepare and execute the proposed changes in a timely manner. Preparation can include the creation of transaction simulations to verify inputs.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Public Communication
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once a change in stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM has been executed, the execution must be publicly communicated to the Sky Ecosystem. This communication may take the form of either (1) a Forum post or (2) inclusion of the execution in an informational dashboard that allows community members to see each stUSDS parameter change executed by the stUSDS BEAM.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Automatic Updates By Operator Hot Wallet
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once the Operator Hot Wallet has been added as an Operator of the stUSDS BEAM (see [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.2 - Operator Hot Wallet](bddf50ca-02ef-4991-abb0-53e09831ee6f)), the stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM must be set by the Operator Hot Wallet. These changes must be based on the update methodology specified in [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3 - Update Methodology](e37d1045-215d-4f85-bbc3-70aa2c1b818b) and the results must be publicly visible on an information dashboard.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Update Methodology
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define the methodology that should be used for determining when and how to update stUSDS parameters with the stUSDS BEAM.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short Term Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Initially when stUSDS parameters are set manually by the Operator Multisig (see [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.2 - Manual Parameter Updates By Operator Multisig](944c2573-1184-4d6b-bbe9-0b84c11956cf)), the Core Council Risk Advisor may deviate from the long term process specified in [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2 - Long Term Process](b349277c-4e61-474a-85bd-18802324a3a6) in their best judgment based on the guidelines specified herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Initial Supply Rate
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The initial value of the `str` parameter must be set extraordinarily high to a value of approximately 40% initially to incentivize deposits. This rate is not sustainable and must be lowered as specified in [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.1.2 - Gradual Reduction In Supply Rate](8441e561-ef59-4a7e-a6d6-438f1bf797be).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Gradual Reduction In Supply Rate
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `str` parameter must be gradually lowered to a more sustainable level as the market approaches the optimal target of 90% utilization.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Growth Of Market Size
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `cap` and `line` parameters must be scaled up over time. By the time the market reaches 300,000,000 USDS the `str` parameter must be normalized to a level that is in line with the Interest Rate Model.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Long Term Process
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Once the process is fully automated (see [A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.3 - Automatic Updates By Operator Hot Wallet](a6e1735f-bd82-4ab6-982b-218013c3455f)) the automated process must fully conform to the specifications herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Conditions For Update
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The stUSDS parameters should be updated if (1) the current Utilization (see [A.4.4.1.2.2.1.4 - Utilization Definition](337c4f67-685f-42bd-8237-553ed913b89f)) deviates from the Utilization as of the last time the stUSDS parameters were set by more than 2.4% and (2) the time since the last update is greater than `tau` (see [A.4.4.1.2.8.1.6 - Tau Definition](4f82fc17-4bcc-4623-b09b-b495c43b06f7)).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Calculations For Update
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The new values for each stUSDS parameter should be set to be as close as possible to the values specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Str Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `str` must be calculated as specified in [A.4.4.1.2.2 - stUSDS Rate](7e51d5a7-0707-4fba-999b-a1becd5f0192).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Duty Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `duty` must be calculated as specified in [A.4.4.1.2.5.1.2 - Rate Setting Formula](05e97d4d-37e2-4ed8-acea-a8728fbe0402).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Cap Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Core Council Risk Advisor must calculate the maximum amount that users can deposit into the stUSDS contract (`cap`) as specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short Term Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: In the short term while Utilization is above 100%, the `cap` must be set to 200,000,000 USDS.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Long Term Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
In the long term, the `cap` must be gradually increased when Utilization is above 85% according to the following formula:

`cap = 1.2 * current SKY borrowing`

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Line Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Core Council Risk Advisor must calculate the maximum amount that users can borrow against their staked SKY (`line`) as specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short Term Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: In the short term while Utilization is above 100%, the `line` must be set to 200,000,000 USDS.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Long Term Calculation
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
In the long term, the `line` must be gradually increased when Utilization is above 85% according to the following formula:

`line = 1.14 * current SKY borrowing`

sections_and_primary_docs.name: SKY-Backed Borrowing Capped OSM Wrapper
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
In order to prevent excessive price spikes if there is high demand for leverage against SKY tokens, which could potentially lead to excessive USDS borrowing against SKY, a wrapper for the SKY OSM contract has been developed.

The wrapper enforces an upper limit on the price of SKY for the purposes of SKY-Backed 
Borrowing, which is set to be the minimum value of:

1. The current price reported by PIP_SKY.

2. The `cap` that has been set on the OSM wrapper contract.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Cap Parameter
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The subdocuments herein further describe the `cap` parameter and the process for its modification.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Definition
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `cap` parameter represents one of the potential inputs for the SKY-Backed Borrowing Capped OSM Wrapper. The OSM wrapper uses the minimum value of the `cap` or the PIP_SKY oracle price to determine the price utilized for SKY-backed borrowing.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Core Facilitator, in consultation with the Core Council Risk Advisor, has the ability to modify the `cap` parameter. The modification of said parameter is pursuant to the Operational Weekly Cycle and can be effected directly via an Executive Vote, without requiring a prior Governance Poll.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Current Value
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The current value of the `cap` parameter is:

• 0.04 USDS.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short Term Transitionary Measures
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define short-term logic for SKY staking rewards pending the implementation of the Sky Treasury Management Function.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short Term USDS Rewards For SKY Stakers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Until the Treasury Management Function (TMF) is fully implemented, USDS rewards for SKY stakers will be temporarily funded by the Smart Burn Engine, subject to the parameters of the Smart Burn Engine; this interim mechanism will be discontinued once the TMF becomes fully operational.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short Term SKY Rewards For SKY Stakers
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Until the Treasury Management Function (TMF) is fully implemented, SKY rewards for SKY stakers will be temporarily funded by SKY from the Protocol Treasury as specified in [A.4.4.1.3.2.1 - Implementation](ca151bc7-87fc-4749-9776-ea4308817e81); this interim mechanism will be discontinued once the TMF becomes fully operational.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Implementation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: SKY rewards for SKY stakers are implemented through the Staking Rewards contract, the Vested Rewards Distribution contract, and the Vesting Stream contract, as specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Staking Rewards Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Staking Rewards contract is the user facing contract that allows SKY stakers to stake their SKY to receive SKY rewards. It maintains the balance of staked SKY receiving SKY rewards for each user and the associated accumulated rewards balance.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Staking Rewards Contract Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The address of the Staking Rewards contract on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xB44C2Fb4181D7Cb06bdFf34A46FdFe4a259B40Fc`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Staking Rewards Contract Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the Staking Rewards contract are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Owner
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The `owner` of the Staking Rewards contract is the contract that has the ability to control administrative functions for the Staking Rewards contract. The value of the `owner` parameter is the `MCD_PAUSE_PROXY`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rewards Distribution Contract Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Rewards Distribution contract address `rewardsDistribution` is the address of the Rewards Distribution contract associated with the Staking Rewards contract. The value of the `rewardsDistribution` parameter is the address of the Rewards Distribution contract specified in [A.4.4.1.3.2.1.2.1 - Rewards Distribution Contract Address](fdebe206-2f58-4056-8adf-c42dffb47026).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rewards Token
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Rewards Token `rewardsToken` is the token that users receive as rewards. The value of the `rewardsToken` parameter is `SKY`, representing SKY Tokens.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Staking Token
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Staking Token `stakingToken` is the token that users stake to earn rewards. The value of the `stakingToken` parameter is `LSSKY`, representing staked SKY Tokens.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rewards Distribution Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Rewards Distribution contract is the contract that handles the regular transfer of reward tokens from the Vesting Stream contract to the Staking Rewards contract for distribution to end users.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rewards Distribution Contract Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The address of the Rewards Distribution contract on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0x675671A8756dDb69F7254AFB030865388Ef699Ee`.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Rewards Distribution Contract Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the Rewards Distribution contract are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Staking Rewards Contract Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Staking Rewards contract address `stakingRewards` is the address of the Staking Rewards contract associated with the Rewards Distribution contract. The value of the `stakingRewards` parameter is the address of the Staking Rewards contract specified in [A.4.4.1.3.2.1.1.1 - Staking Rewards Contract Address](b4989cd9-f45e-4747-8861-fac4175624cc).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Stream Contract Address
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Vesting Stream contract address `dssVest` is the address of the Vesting Stream contract associated with the Rewards Distribution contract. The value of the `dssVest` parameter is the address of the Vesting Stream contract specified in [A.4.4.1.3.2.1.3.1 - Vesting Stream](89155294-6652-481f-938f-a562d5b40e65).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Stream Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Vesting Stream contract manages various vesting streams that vest SKY Tokens from the Protocol Treasury. One of these vesting streams regularly vests SKY Tokens to the Staking Rewards contract.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Stream
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The address of the Vesting Stream contract is the address corresponding to the  `MCD_VEST_SKY_TREASURY` key in the Chainlog.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Stream Contract Parameters
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the vesting stream managed by the Vesting Stream contract that vests SKY Tokens to the Staking Rewards contract are specified in the documents herein.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Duration
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Vesting Duration `vestTau` is the total duration over which the Vesting Total number of tokens are to be vested linearly. The initial value of the `vestTau` parameter is 180 days.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Total
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Vesting Total `vestTot` is the number of rewards tokens to be vested in total over the Vesting Duration. The initial value of the `vestTot` parameter is 1,000,000,000 SKY.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Vesting Stream Parameter Modification
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The parameters of the vesting stream that vests SKY Tokens to be transferred to the Staking Rewards contract may be modified by the Core Facilitator on the recommendation of the Core Council Risk Advisor through the Operational Weekly Cycle, and can be effected directly via an Executive Vote, without a prior Governance Poll. It is expected that the parameters will be reviewed and updated approximately every 90 days.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Source Of SKY Rewards
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
Initially, the SKY Tokens in the Protocol Treasury to fund SKY rewards for SKY stakers will be funded by the 500,000,000 SKY transferred by the Sky Frontier Foundation to the Protocol Treasury.

The `vestTot` and `vestTau` parameters of the Vesting Stream contract are set such that these 500,000,000 SKY will be distributed over three (3) months. After these three (3) months, SKY rewards must be funded by SKY acquired through buybacks and the parameters of the Vesting Stream contract must be adjusted accordingly.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Authorization Of Transfer By Sky Frontier Foundation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Sky Governance hereby confirms that the transfer of the 500,000,000 SKY tokens specified in [A.4.4.1.3.2.2 - Source Of SKY Rewards](349a350c-c9b7-4232-a83f-2fb49b91fc74) is consistent with the terms of the grant to the Sky Frontier Foundation. See [A.2.14.1 - Ecosystem Entity Grants](5d5759e4-8077-4af5-9a1a-eaeab5088dd7).

articles.name: Distribution Of Agent Tokens
articles.content: When Sky invests capital in Agents, the tokens Sky receives in exchange are distributed in accordance with the terms of the Ecosystem Accord between Sky and the respective Agent.

articles.name: Protocol Mechanisms
articles.content: This Article defines maintenance or housekeeping mechanisms and contracts used to administer the Sky Ecosystem.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Token Transfers To Sky
sections_and_primary_docs.content: This Section defines standard procedures for sending tokens to Sky.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Process for Returning Tokens To Sky When No Specific Process Exists
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define processes for sending tokens to Sky. These include the DssBlow2 contract for adding Dai and USDS to the Surplus Buffer, and the Pause Proxy contract for non-stablecoins.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Transfer Of Stablecoins To Sky Protocol
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define the instructions for transferring stablecoins to Sky.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Send Dai Or USDS To DssBlow2 Contract
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Transfers of Dai or USDS that are not required to pay down vault debt should result in an increase to the Sky Protocol Surplus Buffer (see [A.3.5.1 - Surplus Buffer](9782cdc5-c274-45c2-bf4a-690f22c6a294)).
