This process is handled via the DssBlow2 contract, as described in the subdocuments.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: DssBlow2
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Dai or USDS tokens can be transferred to the DssBlow2 contract, MCD_BLOW2, at `0x81EFc7Dd25241acd8E5620F177E42F4857A02B79`. Calling the `blow` function on this contract will cause any Dai or USDS tokens held by it to be added to the Surplus Buffer. During this process, the ERC-20 tokens are burned and the tokens are instead reflected as an internal balance in the MCD_VAT contract.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Unrecoverability Of Non-Dai Or USDS Tokens
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Only Dai or USDS are supported by DssBlow2. Any other tokens sent to DssBlow2 are not recoverable by Sky. Senders bear full responsibility; such transfers are not considered a valid receipt of funds owed.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Transfer Of Other Stablecoins
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Stablecoins other than Dai or USDS cannot be added to the Surplus Buffer via DssBlow2. They should either (1) be converted to Dai or USDS and sent to DssBlow2 or (2) be sent to the Pause Proxy contract without conversion. Receipt of Dai or USDS is Sky’s preferred method of receiving stablecoins.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Transfer Of Non-Stablecoins To Sky Protocol
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein define the instructions for transferring non-stablecoins to Sky.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Send Non-Stablecoins To Pause Proxy
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Transfers of non-stablecoins to Sky should be sent to the Pause Proxy contract.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Pause Proxy
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Pause Proxy Contract, MCD_PAUSE_PROXY, at `0xbe8e3e3618f7474f8cb1d074a26affef007e98fb`, is under the direct control of Sky Governance. Sky Governance may take actions through Executive Votes to interact with the tokens held by the Pause Proxy, including selling these tokens.

name: The Accessibility Scope
content: The Accessibility Scope governs accessibility and distribution efforts, and regulates user-facing frontends.

articles.name: Brand Identity
articles.content: This Article governs the brand identity of Sky.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Brand Identity
sections_and_primary_docs.content: This Section defines the management and use of Sky’s brand identity.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Website And Domain
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The IP rights of the Sky website must be transferred to an entity similar to the Dai Foundation.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Dai
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Dai must remain as a valid token and product, with no actively maintained brand presence beyond community assets, educational material and its token name.

articles.name: Accessibility Communication Channels
articles.content: This Article regulates accessibility assets, including communication channels and Sky’s communication presence on external websites.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Accessibility Communication Channels
sections_and_primary_docs.content: This Section defines rules for managing accessible communication channels to enhance public access to, and interaction with, the Sky Ecosystem. These communication channels are distinct from Sky’s governance-focused communication channels.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: External Platforms
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Sky must support the accessibility of the Sky Ecosystem by paying Ecosystem Actors to maintain accounts and channels on external platforms, such as Twitter and Telegram. These accounts may develop and share Accessibility content that follows the brand guidelines.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Moderation
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Accessibility Communication channels are subject to the moderation policies specified in [A.2.8.1.2 - Moderation](be3da4c5-6882-4694-9ccd-3fa7c5f6e09a).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Budget
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The Accessibility communication channel budget is available to maintain the tasks described in [A.5.2.1 - Accessibility Communication Channels](8942c3c7-61bd-4cf6-a3f5-4366290801b4) and its subdocuments.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Amount
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The Accessibility communication channel budget is:

• 0 USDS per month, implemented with DssVest. 

It is a monthly recurring budget.

articles.name: Accessibility Campaigns
articles.content: This Article regulates accessibility campaigns.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Accessibility Campaigns
sections_and_primary_docs.content: This Section defines infrastructure and processes pertaining to accessibility campaigns.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Early Bird Reward
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
As a part of the brand reveal phase, the Accessibility Facilitators set up an Early Bird Reward System. The Early Bird Reward System rewarded all users signing up before the launch date of USDS and SKY with double the SKY rewards for the first month following launch.

The accumulated SKY rewards were paid out as an airdrop as specified in [A.5.3.1.1.1 - Token Distribution](eaf8cf29-90fd-4b9b-b0a8-02ce8386908c).

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Token Distribution
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
To distribute the Early Bird Reward the following actions have been or will be taken:

• 27,222,832.8 newly minted SKY, equal to 120% of the total estimated Early Bird Reward distributions, was transferred to the multisig wallet operated by the Accessibility Facilitators at the address `0x14D98650d46BF7679BBD05D4f615A1547C87Bf68` on the Ethereum Mainnet.

• The remaining SKY in the `0x14D98650d46BF7679BBD05D4f615A1547C87Bf68` multisig that is not used to fund the MerkleDistributor will be burned by calling the `burn` function on the multisig. The estimated amount of SKY needed to fund the MerkleDistributor is 22,685,694.

articles.name: Location Resilience
articles.content: This Article defines the location-filtering rules applicable to Ecosystem Actors that operate Frontends on behalf of Agents; and Ecosystem Actors that operate Frontends and receive Accessibility Rewards.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Location Resilience
sections_and_primary_docs.content: This Section defines requirements for Ecosystem Actors and Agents to implement limited filtering and full blocking of IPs.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Reduce Exposure To Locations
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Ecosystem Actors in the Sky Ecosystem must actively explore and implement reasonably available options to reduce their infrastructure's exposure to locations identified in the following subdocuments as subject to either IP filtering ("limited filtering") or IP blocking ("full block").

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Consequence For Non-Compliance
sections_and_primary_docs.content: All frontends operated by Ecosystem Actors on behalf of Agents are required to follow the location-filtering rules defined in [A.5.4.1 - Location Resilience](396e9ea2-c964-43be-9164-edb302ab62cf) and its subdocuments. The failure to adhere to these rules will result in penalties.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Limited Filtering Of Features & IPs
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Limited filtering requires frontends operated by Ecosystem Actors to avoid displaying or describing features to users with IPs flagged for limited filtering.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Restricted Features
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The features that must be restricted from being displayed or described to users flagged for Limited Filtering are:

• Staking Rewards

• Sky Savings Rate

• Any other feature related to yield or rewards.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Flagged IPs
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Frontends must develop internal processes to determine their strategy for limited filtering, to ensure optimal balance of resilience and accessibility.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Full Block
sections_and_primary_docs.content: Full block requires frontends operated by Ecosystem Actors to deny service of any kind to users with IPs flagged for Full Block.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Flagged IPs
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
User IPs flagged for Full Block are:

• Cuba

• Iran

• Syria

• North Korea

• Crimea and Sevastopol

• Donetsk People’s Republic

• Luhansk People’s Republic of Ukraine

• Kherson Oblast

• Zaporizhzhia Oblast

articles.name: Short-Term Transitionary Measures
articles.content: This Article defines short term transitionary measures related to the Accessibility Scope for the Sky Ecosystem.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Short-Term Transitionary Measures
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The logic defined in this section are short term transitionary measures related to the Accessibility Scope before budgets or measures can be transitioned to Sky Entities, Agents, or other Ecosystem Actors.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping
sections_and_primary_docs.content:
The Accessibility Facilitators must work with advisors and Ecosystem Actors to implement a liquidity bootstrapping program using up to 20 million USDS and 320 million SKY. These assets can be used for liquidity programs, or for liquidity infrastructure integration costs. Anything leftover from usage in liquidity programs must be returned to Sky control at the end of the bootstrapping program.

The funds can be paid into multisig accounts for future use via executive votes at will by the Accessibility Facilitators.

name: The Agent Scope
content: The Agent Scope regulates all Agents within the Sky Ecosystem and comprises all Agent Artifacts. Each Agent Artifact governs the operations of a particular Agent.

articles.name: Agent Artifacts
articles.content: This Article includes the Artifacts of each Agent.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: List Of Prime Agent Artifacts
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein each set out the unique Artifact for a particular Prime Agent. Prime Agent Artifacts contain all rules, processes, parameters, and information relevant to the Prime Agent. Prime Agent Artifacts are collections of documents that define each Agent’s strategic vision and day-to-day operational logic.

sections_and_primary_docs.name: List Of Executor Agent Artifacts
sections_and_primary_docs.content: The documents herein each set out the Artifacts for Executor Agents. Executor Agent Artifacts contain all rules, processes, parameters, and information relevant to the Agent.
